name: Iran
summary: Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE 'ZERO-CASUALTY' CALIBRATION PARTIALLY BROKE — IRAN'S PRE-DAWN 3 JUNE BARRAGE (13 BALLISTIC MISSILES + 17 DRONES, ~30 PROJECTILES) STILL PRODUCED ZERO US-MILITARY CASUALTIES, BUT A DRONE HIT KUWAIT INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT'S PASSENGER TERMINAL, KILLING ONE CIVILIAN (AN INDIAN NATIONAL) AND INJURING 63 — THE CYCLE'S FIRST DEATHS.** (1) KINETIC ESCALATION (CONFIRMED ACTION): CENTCOM said it 'successfully defeated' the attacks on US forces — Iranian missiles aimed at regional neighbours 'failed to reach their intended targets,' air defences 'downed multiple drones,' no US personnel or assets harmed — then ran self-defence strikes on an Iranian ground-control station on Qeshm Island IN the Strait of Hormuz (and reportedly Goruk). The IRGC claimed it hit 'the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait' and 'the HQ of the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain,' warning any repetition would draw a response 'completely different in scale and nature' — CENTCOM explicitly REJECTED the Fifth-Fleet/airbase claims as FALSE (CLAIMED, US-denied; do NOT elevate). Bahrain's BDF intercepted 3 Iranian missiles + drones aimed at 'civilian sites,' no casualties. Read: Iran aimed at US military targets and missed/was intercepted (per CENTCOM); the lethal damage landed on civilian infrastructure in a Gulf mediator state. The 'no US casualties' calibration HOLDS; the 1 June 'no-casualties-at-all' calibration is GONE — a real step up in scale (30 vs 2 projectiles) and consequence (first deaths). (2) TALKS SUSPENSION HARDENED (Iran CONFIRMED-position / US CLAIMED): Tasnim (3 June) said Iran gave NO response on the text of the understanding and has 'effectively suspended even the exchange of texts through intermediaries until Iran's conditions regarding Lebanon are met'; Trump/Rubio pushed back that talks 'continue at a rapid pace' and there is 'the prospect' Iran could negotiate its nuclear programme. The gap WIDENED vs 1 June. Negotiate-while-bombing, channel further degraded. (3) FIRST INDIAN FATALITY (CONFIRMED, NEW): the civilian killed at Kuwait airport was an Indian national; India's MEA condemned the strike, is assisting the bereaved family/injured, and put 'Indian missions and posts across the region on alert' — the first time the war becomes a domestic Indian political/security event, not only a markets-via-oil event (not yet an evacuation posture). (4) KUWAIT EXPELLED TWO IRANIAN DIPLOMATS (CONFIRMED ACTION): declared two embassy staff persona non grata (24h), condemned 'the criminal Iranian aggression' targeting 'vital and civilian infrastructure' — a Gulf mediator host in open rupture with Tehran, degrading the mediation architecture. (5) HORMUZ unchanged operationally (~5% of pre-war traffic, ~4-5 ships/day); 'complete closure' stays RHETORIC; IRGC '26 vessels under intelligent control' is a CLAIM discrepant with trackers (do NOT elevate). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 3)**: NO trigger moves to active — the barrage was casualty-PRODUCING, but the casualties were third-country civilian, not US/allied-military; the threshold for a qualitatively-larger response was not crossed. Mojtaba-HEU (~Day 13/14, deadline ~June 5) and Iran-Oman-toll (~June 5) STAY watching, deadlines ~48h out; the hardened suspension removes the very forum to negotiate HEU disposal. rial-2M stays watching (no fresh print). Iran's strategic cost rose: it killed a civilian (an Indian) and alienated a Gulf neighbour to make a leverage point against the US. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE PEACE-DIVIDEND NARRATIVE REVERSED OVER THE WEEKEND — IRAN SUSPENDED THE TALKS AND A REAL MULTI-EVENT KINETIC EXCHANGE BROKE THE LULL, BUT EVERY LIMB WAS STILL CALIBRATED (NO CASUALTIES). Three confirmed reversals of the 30 May 'deal a few days away / oil -22% MoM / tail de-priced' world. (1) DIPLOMATIC RUPTURE (CONFIRMED ACTION): on 1 June Iran's negotiating team SUSPENDED 'dialogues and exchange of texts through a mediator' (Tasnim via ToI/Euronews/CNBC/CNN), citing 'the continuation of the Zionist regime's crimes in Lebanon'; FM Araghchi put the doctrine on record — 'the ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all fronts, including Lebanon. Its violation on one front is a violation on all fronts.' The suspension is a PROCEDURAL ACT (Iran stopped exchanging messages), not merely a statement — CONFIRMED. Against it, Trump posted talks are 'continuing, at a rapid pace,' an MOU 'as early as next week' (CLAIMED — US framing). Deal status: SUSPENDED by Iran (CONFIRMED) / 'continuing' (US, CLAIMED) — do NOT encode the deal as dead OR alive. (2) KINETIC EXCHANGE (CONFIRMED ACTION, A2): a genuine tit-for-tat 30 May-1 June — US fired a Hellfire into a commercial ship's engine room enforcing the blockade; Iran shot down a US MQ-1 Predator over international waters (31 May); CENTCOM ran 'measured, deliberate self-defence strikes' on Iranian radar/drone command-and-control sites at Goruk and Qeshm Island (eliminating air defences, a ground-control station, two OWA drones); Iran fired TWO ballistic missiles at US forces in Kuwait (Sun night), BOTH intercepted, no casualties (CENTCOM). CALIBRATION IS THE NUANCE: an unmanned drone, intercepted missiles, zero casualties — escalation-as-leverage (Sadjadpour/Ain al-Assad template), NOT escalation-to-war. The political payload (suspending talks, threatening chokepoints) outweighs the military payload. (3) Attached chokepoint maximalism — Tasnim vowed the 'resistance front' would 'completely close the Strait of Hormuz and activate the Bab al-Mandab' (RHETORIC/THREAT; operationally Hormuz unchanged at ~4 ships/day, CNN '94 days of paralysis'); the IRGC claimed 24 vessels passed under 'intelligent control' (CLAIMED Iran, discrepant with independent ~4/day — do NOT elevate). Oil gapped Brent +7.3% to ~$97 (1 June) then eased to ~$94.5 (2 June). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 2)**: NO trigger moves to active on the kinetic limbs (all calibrated/intercepted/zero-casualty) or the chokepoint THREATS (no operational change). Mojtaba-HEU stays watching (~Day 12 of 14, deadline ~June 5; still no proof-of-life, and the suspension removes the very forum in which HEU disposal would be negotiated — nuclear kept off-table this round per IRNA 'cessation-of-hostilities only'). Iran-Oman toll mechanism stays watching (no permanent toll announced; ~June 5). rial-2M stays watching (no fresh deterioration print; suspension removes the near-term frozen-asset/$12bn upside catalyst — modestly rial-negative at the margin, but still far from 2,000,000/USD). The dominant signal is the diplomatic RUPTURE + oil re-pricing, both encoded heavily; the kinetic exchange weighted as confirmed-but-calibrated, not casualty-producing war. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; covers 28 May afternoon -> 30 May morning). **THE POST-MISSILE WORLD DID NOT ESCALATE — IRAN REVERTED TO THE TABLE AND REFRAMED ITS PRICE AS CASH, NOT NUCLEAR CONCESSIONS. The 28 May calibrated IRGC missile (intercepted over Kuwait, zero casualties) produced NO further missile exchange and NO new airstrikes through 30 May — confirming the 'calibrated/contained' read. The one new kinetic development is a US ENFORCEMENT action, not a strike: on 30 May the US military DISABLED the Gambia-flagged bulk carrier Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman after it tried to enter Iranian ports — the 6th vessel stopped during the blockade (CONFIRMED — Iran International liveblog citing US military). War Secretary Hegseth: the 'blockade is very much still in place,' Hormuz must stay 'an open, toll-free strait' (CONFIRMED). The genuinely new intelligence is a MONEY-FIRST hardening: Iran's semi-official Fars directly contradicted Trump's account of the unsigned 60-day MOU, stating the draft contains NO HEU-destruction clause and that its 'most important part' is the immediate release of $12bn of Iran's frozen assets — with Iran refusing further negotiation until paid (REPORTED — Fars; Iran's framing, contested). Trump insists the ~1,000 lbs HEU 'will be destroyed' and 'no sanctions, no money, no nothing' (CLAIMED — US framing). Both the destruction clause and the $12bn-first demand are CLAIMED by their respective sides until a signed text exists — the one-side-framing pattern running in BOTH directions. On 29 May Iran publicly claimed 'no negotiations' on the nuclear file are taking place (CLAIMED Iran), and Araghchi reaffirmed the enriched-material file is 'postponed/deadlocked.' Sadjadpour re-validated: Iran manages escalation for leverage (frozen-asset relief), not war. TRIGGERS: Mojtaba HEU directive ~Day 9 of 14 (deadline ~June 5), STAYS watching — still no proof-of-life, no state-media confirmation/walk-back. Iran-Oman Hormuz-toll trigger STAYS watching — the toll question is now LIVE friction (US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction Oman over tolls, intel A8) but no permanent toll mechanism has been announced. rial-2M STAYS watching — USD/IRR ~1.34m (TradingEconomics 27 May) to ~1.72m (free-market), well away from the 2,000,000 hyperinflation threshold on either basis. NO trigger moves to active — the dominant signal of the window is a CONFIRMED NON-EVENT of non-escalation.** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **IRAN CROSSED FROM RHETORIC TO ACTION — BUT IN THE CALIBRATED, FACE-SAVING 2020 AIN AL-ASSAD MODE, NOT ESCALATION TO WAR. After 48h of vowing a 'graveyard' without firing a shot, the IRGC fired ONE ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait at ~04:50 local May 28, in response to a SECOND US 'defensive' strike (US downed 4 Iranian one-way attack drones near Hormuz + struck an Iranian ground-control station near Bandar Abbas 'reportedly preparing to launch a fifth' drone). The missile was TELEGRAPHED, INTERCEPTED by Kuwaiti air defenses, ZERO casualties/damage; CENTCOM called it an 'egregious ceasefire violation.' A genuine two-sided kinetic exchange (CONFIRMED ACTION both sides) — the FIRST confirmed Iranian retaliatory strike of this window — but Washington and Tehran 'have not returned to full-scale hostilities and have kept negotiating' (WaPo/PBS). The May 27 'Iran did NOT retaliate' thesis is now HALF-BROKEN (Iran did strike) and HALF-CONFIRMED (it did so in a manner designed not to escalate). DO NOT encode as 'Iran attacks US / war resumes' — tag the SIGNIFICANCE as calibrated/contained (Fact-Checker F1). Iran minimizes the US strike (Tasnim: US 'fired at a barren area near Bandar Abbas'; IRGC Navy 'warning shot at a US oil tanker').** Simultaneously the DEAL HARDENED INTO DEADLOCK: an MOU to extend the ceasefire 60 days has reportedly been REACHED but awaits Trump's signature (REPORTED, single US-official source via US News — terms: Hormuz open 'with no tolls,' Iran clears its mines, US lifts the port blockade + 'some sanctions waivers'); against that, Trump hardened publicly — 'No sanctions, no money, no nothing,' Iran's ~1,000 lbs HEU 'will be destroyed' (US, Iran, or 'another acceptable location'), and he OPPOSES Russia/China taking custody ('No dust, no dollars'); the White House dismissed an Iranian-state-TV draft framework (gradual Oman-mediated Hormuz reopening, US withdrawal, blockade lifting) as a 'complete fabrication'; FM Araghchi declared the enriched-uranium file at 'DEADLOCK,' postponed to later stages, while Shamkhani again called US control of the nuclear program a 'fantasy.' Net: ceasefire-extension limb incrementally more concrete (a document exists) while the nuclear + sanctions limbs HARDENED — and the live exchange cuts against signature. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (May 28)**: the second-commercial-vessel-seizure trigger (by 2026-05-28) EXPIRES UNTRIPPED / RESOLVED — no new Iranian seizure; the week's Gulf story is the kinetic exchange, not a seizure (A6). All deal-acceptance triggers STAY watching (MOU unsigned; nuclear at deadlock). Mojtaba-HEU stays watching (Day 7 of 14, state media still silent). rial-2M stays watching (no fresh print this window; carry ~1,720,000/USD, away from threshold). **NO trigger moves to 'active' on the missile** — it was calibrated, intercepted, zero-casualty, both sides kept negotiating; this is escalation MANAGEMENT (Sadjadpour), not escalation to war. **The market's verdict is the tell: Brent spiked only +2-3% then faded to ~$93-96 (down >10% since May 18) and gold FELL to a two-month low breaking $4,400 — on a day with a live US-Iran missile exchange.** PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27; ~48h coverage). **IRAN WAS STRUCK BUT DID NOT RETALIATE — THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT NON-EVENT. The US conducted CONFIRMED self-defense strikes inside southern Iran Monday May 25 (two IRGC fast boats laying mines in Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site); Iran's FM called it 'bad faith' and a ceasefire violation and VOWED retaliation — but as of May 27 has carried out NO retaliatory military action (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT; Euronews: 'only threats and warnings'). The rhetoric escalated (IRGC Navy deputy Akbarzadeh: 'turn the area from Chabahar to Mahshahr into a graveyard for aggressors') but the tell is inside the bravado — Akbarzadeh himself called renewed full war 'remote.' Maximal rhetoric, calibrated restraint; the trigger that matters — an actual Iranian retaliatory strike — has NOT fired. DO NOT elevate the 'graveyard' line to operational signal.** The deal moved 'closer than at any point' on paper (Trump 'largely negotiated') but Lebanon is now an EXPLICIT named blocker (Iran insists any deal must end the war 'on all fronts including Lebanon') alongside Hormuz and nuclear; fresh Iranian hard lines: Deputy FM Bagheri Kani 'the two sides had not yet agreed on lifting the blockade'; Khamenei adviser Shamkhani dismissed US control over Iran's nuclear programme as a 'fantasy'; FM Araghchi unsure a deal is imminent. The rial firmed to ~1,720,000/USD (+0.12% d/d, -3.21% on the week — rial stronger) on deal anticipation — moving AWAY from the 2,000,000 hyperinflation trigger even THROUGH the strikes (the parallel market did not panic). **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: rial-2M stays watching (firmer, away from threshold); Mojtaba-HEU stays watching (Day 6 of 14, state media silent); the second-commercial-vessel-seizure trigger stays watching (~Day 13/14, on track to expire untripped May 28 — the May 26-27 maritime story is US strikes on IRGC boats, NOT an Iranian seizure); all Iran-retaliatory-strike triggers (Tangsiri etc.) STAY WATCHING (Iran did NOT act). No weight bump for the 'graveyard' rhetoric. **MAY 25-27: IRAN WAS STRUCK (CONFIRMED) AND DID NOT RETALIATE (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT); IRAN STILL LAYING MINES IN HORMUZ (CONFIRMED via the US strike on the mine-laying boats) IS THE ONE CONFIRMED IRANIAN ACTION.** PRIOR (May 25): **IRAN DREW THE 'NOT IMMINENT' LINE IN PUBLIC AND PUT ITS ECONOMIC PRINCIPALS ON A PLANE TO DOHA. FM SPOKESMAN BAGHAEI: 'WE HAVE REACHED CONCLUSIONS ON MANY ISSUES… BUT NO ONE CAN CLAIM AN IMMINENT AGREEMENT IS ABOUT TO BE SIGNED'; NUCLEAR 'ISN'T PART OF DISCUSSIONS AT THIS STAGE'; WAR MUST END 'ON ALL FRONTS, INCLUDING LEBANON'; AND ON WASHINGTON — 'WITHIN JUST A FEW HOURS YOU CAN ENCOUNTER COMPLETELY DIFFERENT, AND IN MANY CASES CONTRADICTORY, POSITIONS' (IRAN PUBLICLY WEAPONISING TRUMP'S OWN VOLATILITY AS A NEGOTIATING POINT). A SENIOR IRANIAN DELEGATION — SPEAKER GHALIBAF, FM ARAGHCHI, CENTRAL-BANK GOVERNOR HEMMATI — ARRIVED IN DOHA MAY 25 TO WORK HORMUZ + HEU + FROZEN-ASSET UNFREEZING WITH QATAR'S PM; THE CENTRAL-BANK GOVERNOR'S PRESENCE CONFIRMS THE FROZEN-ASSET MECHANICS ARE NOW A LIVE TECHNICAL WORKSTREAM (FARS: 'WASHINGTON HAS AGREED TO RELEASE PART OF TEHRAN'S FROZEN OVERSEAS ASSETS' IN EXCHANGE FOR LIFTING HORMUZ RESTRICTIONS). THE RIAL FIRMED TO ~1,707,000/USD (-~4.8% ON THE WEEK, RIAL STRENGTHENED) ON DEAL ANTICIPATION — MOVING AWAY FROM THE 2,000,000 HYPERINFLATION TRIGGER, WHICH HEMMATI (NOW IN DOHA) IS THE CBI PRINCIPAL MANDATED TO DEFEND. (1) **Baghaei 'understanding but not imminent' (CONFIRMED — NBC News, CNBC, Tribune India, Yahoo/AP, Gazeta Express, NewsNation, May 25)**; Rubio matched the cooling: talks 'still a work in progress,' 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz (CNN liveblog May 25). Both sides converging on 'not imminent.' (2) **Doha delegation + money track (CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN, May 25)**: Ghalibaf/Araghchi/Hemmati met Qatar's PM; agenda explicitly Hormuz + highly-enriched uranium + frozen-asset unfreezing; Qatar in an 'increasingly active role' beyond pure messenger. (3) **Mojtaba HEU-disposal contradiction persists (REPORTED vs CLAIMED-CONTESTED)**: US sources (Axios) say Iranians gave assurances 'verbal and in writing' that any permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium and that Mojtaba 'endorsed the broad template' — against Baghaei 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage.' Iranian state media has STILL neither confirmed nor walked back the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive — Day 4 of the 14-day window; the negative-space signal persists. Pezeshkian said Iran 'is ready to assure the world it is not pursuing nuclear weapons' — a non-proliferation assurance compatible with both keeping and disposing, resolving nothing. (4) **Trump 'mandatory' Abraham Accords condition (RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND — CONFIRMED Trump posts; no signed accords)**: Trump declared it 'should be mandatory' that six Muslim nations (Saudi, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan, Turkey) 'simultaneously sign onto the Abraham Accords,' starting with Saudi/Qatar, and invited Iran into the 'World Coalition' if it signs — bolting Arab-Israeli normalisation onto the Hormuz/nuclear de-escalation deal and vastly increasing its degrees of freedom; per Axios, the leaders met it with 'silence on the line.' (5) **Iran drone claim (CLAIMED, low significance)**: Iran claimed downing an Israeli 'Orbiter' surveillance drone over Hormozgan province May 24; IDF 'not familiar.' One short signal, no weight change. **Structural read**: Iran is hardened on Hormuz sovereignty + Lebanon-as-precondition, cooling on timeline (the 'not imminent' floor is now actively defended in public), deflecting on nuclear, and has bolted on an explicit economic/money track (Hemmati in Doha) — the same coordinated-by-function posture with a frozen-asset workstream now live. The tape priced de-escalation (rial firmer); the diplomatic text accumulated unresolved contradictions plus a maximalist new condition. **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION; DAY 87 IS RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC POSTURE, NOT CONFIRMED KINETIC ACTION.** PRIOR (May 24): **THE FARS REBUTTAL IS THE CLEANEST SINGLE ARCHITECTURAL DISPUTE OF THE DEAL CYCLE: IRAN'S SEMI-STATE IRGC-LINKED OUTLET STATED 'THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL STAY UNDER IRAN'S MANAGEMENT' AND THAT VESSEL COUNTS RETURNING TO PRE-WAR LEVELS 'IN NO WAY MEANS FREE PASSAGE AS IT EXISTED BEFORE THE WAR' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS TRUMP'S 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL BE OPENED' FRAMING. BAGHAEI ANCHORED 30-60 DAY MOU TIMELINE 'ONLY BEGINS AFTER DOCUMENT IS FORMALLY AGREED'; NUCLEAR ISSUES 'NOT A FOCUS OF THE PEACE TALKS AT THIS STAGE'; IRAN 'NOT YET ACCEPTED ANY ACTIONS ON ITS NUCLEAR PROGRAMME' — IMPLICIT REJECTION OF US ADMIN 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED TO HEU DISPOSAL' FRAMING. PEZESHKIAN'S 'EFFECTIVE AND PROFESSIONAL MONITORING AND CONTROL MECHANISMS' FORMULATION LANDS CLOSER TO FARS THAN TO TRUMP.** (1) **Fars News Agency (semi-official IRGC-linked) Sunday May 24 (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel May 23 liveblog primary, The Hill aggregator)**: 'Although Iran has agreed to allow the number of passing vessels to return to pre-war levels, this in no way means free passage as it existed before the war.' Iran's primary public position on the leak text. (2) **Baghaei (via Press TV / IRNA / Mehr News, CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: 'nuclear issues are not a focus of the peace talks at this stage'; Iran 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme'; '30 to 60 days' window for finalizing MOU details ONLY BEGINS ONCE THE DOCUMENT IS FORMALLY AGREED — operational floor under any US 'within 7 days' framing. (3) **Pezeshkian (Mehr News May 24)**: 'navigation conditions in the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal' with 'effective and professional monitoring and control mechanisms… within the framework of international law' — formulation lands closer to Fars's 'management' framing than to Trump's 'opened' framing, suggesting the civilian government is coordinating with the IRGC-linked outlet on the sovereignty axis while engaging procedurally with Pakistan/Qatar. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 3 of 14** toward June 5 confirmation/walk-back deadline. NO Iranian state-media outlet has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leak. Baghaei's 'nuclear issues not being discussed' is implicit alignment with directive substance without explicit attribution. (5) **Munir COAS Tehran visit operationally concluded Saturday May 23** — ISPR 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' is Pakistan-side framing only, not validated by Iranian-side equivalent statement. Iranian-side framing (Baghaei) was the more guarded 'reduction in disputes, but there are still issues that need to be discussed through mediators.' (6) **Qatari negotiating team in Tehran since Friday May 22** (REPORTED — Reuters via JPost) in coordination with US — dual mediation architecture (Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel) now operationally explicit with parallel delegations. (7) **Tasnim 'preparing for resuming fighting' military-source piece NOT FOLLOWED UP Sunday** — modest cooling from Saturday's aggressive framing, consistent with the deal track still being live. (8) **Iran's institutional architecture now generates four parallel postures coordinated by function**: Fars sovereignty-maximalist + Baghaei procedural + Pezeshkian formula-finder + Mojtaba directive-via-leak. Hormuz sovereignty language is the ONE axis where the regime is publicly unified-defiant. (9) **AIS shipping data**: vessel transits remain ~5-6/day vs 138/day historical baseline — Fars's 'agreed to allow vessel counts to return to pre-war levels' is a forward-looking commitment with no operational reopening through May 24 evening Tehran time. (10) **Rial AlanChand May 22 IRR/INR 18,600** (+0.54% d/d); cross-implied IRR/USD ~1,780-1,810K — broadly consistent with May 15-18 last-verified 1,800-1,825K range. 2M trigger remains untriggered; deal-anticipation continues to cap weakening pace. **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION** — Iran response from operational architecture is restrained (no Sunday Tasnim follow-up, no IRGC global-offensive amplification, no parliamentary 90% rhetoric reprise). **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's adoption of literally the same 'time is on our side' language within 24 hours of his own 'decide tomorrow' framing. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum institutional fanout** with the four-axis coordinated posture now publicly visible. **The single most architecturally consequential dispute in the deal text**: if MOU operationally institutionalises Iran's selective-management role, Rubio's May 21 red line is breached in text; if the MOU language closes that loophole, Iran's domestic propaganda position is breached. BOTH sides currently believe their version is in the leak text — either ambiguity by design or a structural mismatch waiting to collapse. PRIOR (May 23): IRAN'S INSTITUTIONAL ARCHITECTURE FANNED OUT AT MAXIMUM AMPLITUDE WHILE PAKISTAN'S COAS MUNIR WAS IN TEHRAN: PEZESHKIAN/ARAGHCHI ENGAGED PROCEDURALLY, GHALIBAF + UN MISSION RHETORICALLY HARDENED ('US NOT HONEST PARTNER' / 'EXCESSIVE DEMANDS'), TASNIM REPORTED 'IRAN PREPARING FOR RESUMING FIGHTING,' MOJTABA LEAK-ARCHITECTURE DIRECTIVE STAYS UNCONFIRMED-AND-UNWALKED. BAGHAEI ANCHORED FIRST PUBLICLY-ARTICULATED IRANIAN TIMELINE: 30-60 DAYS FOR MOU FINALIZATION.** (1) **Munir COAS visit May 22-23**: Pakistan COAS Field Marshal Asim Munir conducted ~24-hour Tehran visit, met Pezeshkian + Ghalibaf + Araghchi + Momeni (CONFIRMED — Al Jazeera primary, ISPR primary, multi-source). ISPR readout claimed 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' (REPORTED — ISPR-sourced framing). Iranian official quoted by Al Jazeera described Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — dual-channel architecture (Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel) now operationally explicit. (2) **Iranian institutional counter-signal at maximum amplitude while Munir is in Tehran**: FM Baghaei stated 'deep and significant disagreements remain' (Al Jazeera primary, IRNA); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf — described in Al Jazeera coverage as Iran's 'top negotiator' — publicly said US is 'not an honest negotiating partner'; Iran UN mission in NY accused Washington of 'excessive demands' pushing talks toward collapse; Tasnim (military source primary) reported May 23 'Iran is preparing for resuming fighting with the United States and Israel.' (3) **Baghaei publicly framed deal timeline: '30 to 60 days' for finalizing MOU details** (Al Jazeera May 23 primary, Haaretz, IRNA) — first publicly-articulated Iranian deal timeline of the cycle. Structurally LONGER than Trump's 'every day better' framing AND Al Arabiya leak's 'within 7 days' architecture. The cross-side timeline gap is the structural mismatch. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 2 of 14** toward June 5 confirmation/walk-back deadline. NO Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive. Gold market priced the directive as material with Thursday's $4,500 break; Trump's softer 'satisfactorily handled' verbal formulation on uranium (vs May 16 letter's 'full dismantlement') suggests US verbally accommodating directive's substance. (5) **IRGC May 23 claim of 25 vessels transited Hormuz** in past 24h 'with IRGC Navy coordination and security' — second consecutive daily claim in 25-35 vessel range (CLAIMED — IRGC primary; contradicted by Bloomberg/Lloyd's/UKMTO continuing to characterize traffic as 'largely frozen'; AWRP ~1% holding inconsistent with claim of 25 daily safe transits). The claim is propaganda lock-in for Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' architecture Rubio's May 21 red line explicitly rejected. (6) **CBS reports US military/intel personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans** in anticipation of renewed strikes (REPORTED, decision 'yet to be made'); symmetrically Iran's Tasnim military source confirms preparing for resumed fighting — both sides administratively pre-positioning while procedural mediation moves. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum institutional fanout** — civilian-government (Pezeshkian/Araghchi/Baghaei) procedural + legislative-conservative (Ghalibaf) rhetorical-maximalist + military-IRGC (Tasnim) kinetic-ready + supreme-leader (Mojtaba leak) nuclear-foreclosure = coordinated multi-track posture, not factional split. Iran signaling it can accept partial deal without conceding sovereign maximalism on any of four axes. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's softer uranium framing ('satisfactorily handled' vs May 16 letter 'full dismantlement') — Iran did not have to publicly concede the nuclear track and the US has verbally accommodated. **The 30-60-day Baghaei timeline is long enough to push deal past Israeli unilateral-risk window (June 5) AND past second-IC-departure window (June 21) — Iran buying time inside deal-protected window to consolidate positions.** **NEW trigger architecture**: deal architecture deliverable inside 7-day window (by 2026-05-30) = Trump's faster track wins; inside 30-day window (by 2026-06-22) = Baghaei anchor operational; no deliverable by 60 days (2026-07-22) = Sadjadpour structural-mismatch confirmed. PRIOR (May 22): **MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S FIRST POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED MAY 21 — ENRICHED URANIUM (60%) ORDERED TO REMAIN STRICTLY INSIDE IRANIAN TERRITORY. DIRECTLY FORECLOSES TRUMP'S MAY 16 LETTER NUCLEAR BASIS FOR A DEAL. PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA MAY 22 ADDRESSES HORMUZ/CEASEFIRE/SANCTIONS BUT NOT URANIUM — THE ASYMMETRIC PATH IRAN APPEARS TO WANT.** Across May 20-22: (1) **Mojtaba directive (REPORTED — Reuters wire via Algemeiner/Kathmandu Post/CDM Press May 21)**: anonymous Iranian sources said Mojtaba ordered Iran's stockpile of uranium enriched to 60% to 'remain strictly inside Iranian territory' — 'the Supreme Leader's directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country.' Sources framed reasoning: Iran would be 'more vulnerable to future attacks' if material left. **Structurally the first publicly-attributed substantive policy directive from Mojtaba since his appointment in March, surfaced AT THE EXACT MOMENT the Pakistan-mediated framework is moving** — timing is consistent with regime-internal signal to Trump that the nuclear track is foreclosed independent of whatever ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions architecture Pakistan can deliver. (2) **Mojtaba public-silence trigger advances to Day 15** with the public-silence pattern intact but the leak-architecture-voice pattern now operative. recent_signals.headline: **IRAN PUBLICLY DEFENDED THE 'NOT IMMINENT' FLOOR AND DEPLOYED ITS ECONOMIC PRINCIPALS TO DOHA. SENIOR DELEGATION (GHALIBAF, ARAGHCHI, CENTRAL-BANK GOVERNOR HEMMATI) IN DOHA FOR HORMUZ + HEU + FROZEN-ASSET TALKS WITH QATAR'S PM. RIAL FIRMED TO ~1,707,000/USD ON DEAL HOPE, MOVING AWAY FROM THE 2M TRIGGER.** (1) **Baghaei 'understanding but not imminent' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC / OFFICIAL POSITION — NBC News May 25 primary, CNBC, Tribune India, Yahoo/AP, Gazeta Express, NewsNation)**: 'It's true that we have reached conclusions on many issues under discussion, but no one can claim that this means an imminent agreement is about to be signed.' Reiterated nuclear programme 'isn't part of discussions at this stage'; restated the war must end 'on all fronts, including Lebanon'; and — the cleanest new Iranian-side intelligence — said of Washington: 'within just a few hours you can encounter completely different, and in many cases contradictory, positions,' publicly weaponising Trump's Saturday→Sunday→Monday oscillation as a negotiating point and a justification for Iran's 30-60-day procedural floor. Rubio matched the cooling (CNN liveblog May 25): negotiations 'still a work in progress,' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz. Both sides converging on 'not imminent.' (2) **Doha delegation + frozen-asset money track (CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews May 25 primary, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN)**: a high-level Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, including FM Abbas Araghchi and Central Bank of Iran governor Abdolnaser Hemmati, arrived in Doha May 25 for talks focused on 'issues related to the Strait of Hormuz and highly enriched uranium' and the unfreezing of Iranian assets, meeting Qatar's Prime Minister. Per Fars, 'Washington has agreed to release part of Tehran's frozen overseas assets' in exchange for lifting Hormuz restrictions and the naval blockade. **The presence of the central-bank governor confirms the frozen-asset mechanics are now a live, technical negotiating workstream** — not just rhetoric — and a material operational upgrade of the Qatar track (yesterday a Qatari team was in Tehran; today Iran's economic and diplomatic principals are in Doha). (3) **Mojtaba HEU-disposal contradiction persists (REPORTED vs CLAIMED-CONTESTED)**: US sources (Axios) say the Iranians gave assurances 'verbal and in writing' that any subsequent permanent deal will include the 'disposal' of all enriched uranium (higher and lower levels), and that Mojtaba Khamenei 'endorsed the broad template' (final approval open). Against this, Baghaei said nuclear 'isn't part of discussions at this stage.' **Iranian state media has STILL neither confirmed nor walked back the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive — Day 4 of the 14-day window** (toward June 5); the negative-space signal continues. Pezeshkian separately said Iran 'is ready to assure the world it is not pursuing nuclear weapons' — a non-proliferation assurance rhetorically compatible with both keeping and disposing of the stockpile, i.e., it resolves nothing. (4) **Trump 'mandatory' Abraham Accords condition bolted onto the deal (RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND — CONFIRMED Trump Truth Social posts via NPR May 25, WaPo, CNBC, ToI, Axios; no signed accords, no named-country acceptance)**: Trump wrote 'Negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding nicely!' and declared it 'should be mandatory' that six Muslim nations — Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan, Turkey — 'simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords,' starting with Saudi and Qatar, and invited Iran to join the 'World Coalition' if it signs. Per Axios's Ravid, Trump floated this on Saturday's leaders' call; the reaction was 'silence on the line.' This is the operative emergence of the previously-flagged Lindsey Graham 'expansionist' vector — now Trump policy — and it vastly increases the deal's degrees of freedom. (5) **Iran drone claim (CLAIMED, low significance)**: Iranian media reported the military intercepted an Israeli 'Orbiter' surveillance drone over Hormozgan province (near the Hormuz coast) May 24; the IDF said it was 'not familiar with the incident.' Intelligence value low; if true it would indicate continued Israeli ISR over southern Iran during the negotiation pause. One short signal, NO weight change. (6) **Rial firmer (REPORTED — operational parallel-market data)**: AlanChand/market May 24 put USD at ~1,707,000 IRR, down ~4.8% on the week (rial strengthened) versus the ~1,780-1,810K range carried May 24, on deal anticipation. Six-month change remains ~+50% (rial weaker yoy) but near-term direction reversed; the 2,000,000/USD black-market hyperinflation trigger remains untriggered and the linear ETA has pushed further out. Hemmati — now in Doha negotiating the frozen-asset release — is the central-bank principal whose mandate is to defend it. **Vali Nasr 'both sides slow' thesis reinforced**: Trump held 'no rush' Monday, Baghaei publicly defended the 30-60-day procedural floor and weaponised Trump's own volatility. **Sadjadpour coordinated-multi-axis posture now has an explicit economic axis** (Hemmati in Doha): Iran is signalling it can sign a partial, sequenced deal (Hormuz + assets + ceasefire) while keeping nuclear and Hormuz-management ambiguous and Lebanon a hard precondition. **MAY 25 IS RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC POSTURE, NOT CONFIRMED KINETIC ACTION.**

recent_signals.headline: **FARS REBUTTAL DAY: IRAN'S SEMI-STATE IRGC-LINKED OUTLET STATED 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL STAY UNDER IRAN'S MANAGEMENT'; VESSEL COUNTS RETURNING TO PRE-WAR LEVELS 'IN NO WAY MEANS FREE PASSAGE AS IT EXISTED BEFORE THE WAR' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS TRUMP'S 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL BE OPENED.' BAGHAEI: NUCLEAR 'NOT A FOCUS OF PEACE TALKS AT THIS STAGE'; 30-60 DAY MOU TIMELINE 'ONLY BEGINS AFTER DOCUMENT FORMALLY AGREED.' PEZESHKIAN FORMULATION ('EFFECTIVE AND PROFESSIONAL MONITORING AND CONTROL MECHANISMS') LANDS CLOSER TO FARS THAN TO TRUMP. MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION.** (1) **Fars News Agency (semi-official IRGC-linked) Sunday May 24** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel May 23 liveblog primary, The Hill, Pravda Trump aggregator): 'Although Iran has agreed to allow the number of passing vessels to return to pre-war levels, this in no way means free passage as it existed before the war.' Iran's primary public position on the leak text. Fars also called Trump's Saturday 'largely negotiated' Truth Social post 'inconsistent with reality.' (2) **Baghaei (via Press TV / IRNA / Mehr News, CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: 'nuclear issues are not a focus of the peace talks at this stage'; Iran 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme'; '30 to 60 days' window for finalizing MOU details ONLY BEGINS once the document is formally agreed — operational floor under any US 'within 7 days' framing. **Direct contradiction of US admin senior source 'in principle agreed to HEU disposal' framing.** (3) **Pezeshkian (Mehr News May 24)**: 'navigation conditions in the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal' with 'effective and professional monitoring and control mechanisms… within the framework of international law' — formulation lands closer to Fars 'management' framing than to Trump 'opened' framing. Civilian government coordinating with IRGC-linked outlet on sovereignty axis. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 3 of 14** toward June 5 deadline — NO Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back. Baghaei's implicit alignment with directive substance, no explicit attribution. (5) **Iran's 60-day MOU timeline now operationally embedded in leak text reconstruction** (REPORTED — Axios Ravid primary; FT, Al Jazeera, TOI, Xinhua secondary): (a) 60-day duration extendable by mutual consent; (b) Hormuz reopened to pre-war vessel counts with Iran clearing mines, US lifting naval blockade, 'relief for performance' principle; (c) sanctions waivers during 60-day window; (d) Iranian frozen-asset unfreezing (Iran wants immediate, US insists post-verification); (e) Nuclear track: Iran commits never to pursue weapons + negotiate enrichment suspension + negotiate HEU removal, details deferred to 60-day window; (f) Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire integrated into MOU. Baghaei specifically anchored: the 30-60 days 'only begins once document is formally agreed upon.' (6) **Munir COAS Tehran visit operationally concluded Saturday May 23** — Iranian-side framing (Baghaei: 'reduction in disputes, but issues remain to be discussed through mediators') notably more guarded than Pakistan ISPR 'encouraging progress.' (7) **Qatari negotiating team in Tehran since Friday May 22** (REPORTED — Reuters via JPost) — dual-channel architecture operationally explicit with parallel delegations. Pezeshkian's bilateral with Iraqi PM Al-Zaidi on Saturday + Pakistan COAS Munir + Qatari team = three parallel mediation tracks active in Tehran simultaneously. (8) **Tasnim 'preparing for resuming fighting' military-source piece NOT FOLLOWED UP Sunday** — modest cooling from Saturday's aggressive framing; deal track still live. (9) **IRGC global-offensive rhetoric NOT amplified Sunday** — consistent with regime ceasefire-track preservation while preserving sovereignty floor. (10) **Iran's four-axis institutional architecture publicly visible**: Fars sovereignty-maximalist + Baghaei procedural + Pezeshkian formula-finder + Mojtaba directive-via-leak. Hormuz sovereignty is the ONE axis where regime is publicly unified-defiant. (11) **AIS shipping data unchanged**: ~5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical baseline; Fars's 'agreed to allow pre-war vessel counts' is forward-looking commitment with no operational reopening through Sunday evening Tehran time. (12) **Rial AlanChand May 22 IRR/INR 18,600** (+0.54% d/d); cross-implied IRR/USD ~1,780-1,810K — consistent with last-verified 1,800-1,825K range; deal-anticipation continues to cap weakening pace; 2M trigger untriggered. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's adoption of literally the same 'time is on our side' language within 24 hours of his own 'decide tomorrow' framing — the cross-side timeline gap collapsed not by Iran moving fast but by Trump moving slow. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum institutional fanout** with four-axis coordinated posture now publicly visible. **The single most architecturally consequential dispute in the deal text**: Fars 'management' vs Trump 'opened' is the public verbalisation of exactly the Iran-Oman selective-toll mechanism that activated the May 22 toll-mechanism trigger (Day 3 of 14, by June 5). If MOU operationally institutionalises Iran's selective-management role, Rubio's May 21 red line is breached in text; if MOU language closes that loophole, Iran's domestic propaganda position is breached. BOTH sides currently believe their version is in the leak text.

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN'S INSTITUTIONAL ARCHITECTURE FANNED OUT AT MAXIMUM AMPLITUDE WHILE PAKISTAN'S COAS MUNIR WAS IN TEHRAN: PEZESHKIAN/ARAGHCHI ENGAGED PROCEDURALLY, GHALIBAF/UN MISSION RHETORICALLY HARDENED, TASNIM REPORTED 'IRAN PREPARING FOR RESUMING FIGHTING.' BAGHAEI ANCHORED FIRST PUBLICLY-ARTICULATED IRANIAN TIMELINE: 30-60 DAYS FOR MOU FINALIZATION. MOJTABA DIRECTIVE DAY 2 OF 14: NO STATE-MEDIA CONFIRMATION OR WALK-BACK.** (1) **Munir COAS Tehran visit May 22-23** (CONFIRMED ACTION — Al Jazeera primary, ARY News, ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel multi-source); met Pezeshkian + Ghalibaf + Araghchi + Momeni. ISPR readout: 'short but highly productive' with 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' on ending the war (REPORTED framing). (2) **Iranian institutional counter-signal at maximum amplitude**: FM Baghaei said 'deep and significant disagreements remain' (Al Jazeera primary, IRNA); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf publicly stated US is 'not an honest negotiating partner' (Al Jazeera primary); Iran UN mission in NY accused Washington of 'excessive demands' pushing talks toward collapse (Al Jazeera primary); Tasnim News Agency reported May 23, citing a military source, that 'Iran is preparing for resuming fighting with the United States and Israel' (Haaretz liveblog corroboration, Tasnim primary). **The Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame is now at maximum institutional fanout** — civilian-government (Pezeshkian/Araghchi/Baghaei) procedural + legislative-conservative (Ghalibaf) rhetorical-maximalist + military-IRGC (Tasnim) kinetic-ready + supreme-leader (Mojtaba leak) nuclear-foreclosure = COORDINATED MULTI-TRACK POSTURE BY FUNCTION, NOT FACTIONAL SPLIT. (3) **Baghaei publicly framed deal timeline: '30 to 60 days' for finalizing MOU details** (Al Jazeera May 23 primary, Haaretz, IRNA) — first publicly-articulated Iranian deal timeline of cycle. Structurally LONGER than Trump's 'every day better' framing AND Al Arabiya leak's 'within 7 days' architecture. **The 30-60-day timeline is also long enough to push deal past Israeli unilateral-risk window (June 5) AND past second-IC-departure window (June 21) — Iran buying time inside deal-protected window.** Iran 'focused on finalizing memorandum of understanding with U.S.' but explicitly noted 'a conclusion would not be reached if the US sought to delve into details about highly enriched uranium in Iran' — structurally consistent with Mojtaba directive's substance without explicit attribution. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 2 of 14**: NO Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive through May 23. The Daily Sabah May 22 piece 'Iran supreme leader vows to keep enriched uranium in country' amplifies the original Reuters wire without adding independent sourcing. **The market has priced the directive as material**: gold fell to $4,510.60 Thursday on reports the directive was complicating the deal track. Trump's softer 'satisfactorily handled' formulation on uranium suggests US verbally accommodating directive's substance without formal deal. (5) **Dual-channel architecture operationally explicit**: Al Jazeera May 23 quoted an Iranian official describing Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel is now-explicit architecture. (6) **IRGC May 23 claim: 25 vessels transited Hormuz** in past 24h with IRGC coordination/security (CLAIMED — IRGC primary, Al Jazeera secondary). Second consecutive daily claim in 25-35 range. Contradicted by Bloomberg/Lloyd's/UKMTO (continued 'largely frozen' framing); AWRP ~1% holding inconsistent with claim. The claim is propaganda lock-in for Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' architecture Rubio's May 21 red line rejected. (7) **CBS reports US military/intel personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans** (REPORTED, decision 'yet to be made'); symmetrically Tasnim military-source 'preparing for resumed fighting' — both sides administratively pre-positioning while procedural mediation moves through Munir-COAS channel. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's softer uranium framing — Iran did not have to publicly concede the nuclear track and US has verbally accommodated. **Falsifiable architecture**: deal deliverable inside 7 days (by 2026-05-30) = Trump's faster track wins, Iran was bluffing on 30-60-day; deliverable inside 30-60 days = Baghaei anchor operational; no deliverable by 2026-07-22 = Sadjadpour structural-mismatch confirmed.

recent_signals.headline: **MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S FIRST SUBSTANTIVE POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED MAY 21 — URANIUM ENRICHED TO 60% TO 'REMAIN STRICTLY INSIDE IRANIAN TERRITORY.' DIRECTLY FORECLOSES TRUMP'S MAY 16 NUCLEAR ULTIMATUM. PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA ADDRESSES HORMUZ/CEASEFIRE/SANCTIONS — BUT NOT URANIUM. IRGC THREATENED 'GLOBAL OFFENSIVE' MAY 21 (RHETORIC, NO ACTION). MOJTABA STATE-MEDIA SILENCE DAY 15; THE DIRECTIVE IS NOW THE VOICE.** (1) **Mojtaba directive (REPORTED — Reuters wire via Algemeiner/Kathmandu Post/CDM Press May 21)**: anonymous Iranian sources said Mojtaba ordered that Iran's stockpile of uranium enriched to 60% 'remain strictly inside Iranian territory' — 'the Supreme Leader's directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country.' Reasoning per sources: Iran would be 'more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel' if material left. **Structurally the first publicly-attributed substantive policy directive from Mojtaba since his appointment in March, surfaced AT THE EXACT MOMENT the Pakistan-mediated framework is moving** — regime-internal signal to Trump that the nuclear track is foreclosed independent of operational deliverable architecture. (2) **Pakistan track operationally active**: Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi met FM Araghchi in Tehran multiple times May 20-22 (CONFIRMED — Washington Times May 22, IRNA primary). Al Arabiya English May 22 leaked what it called the 'final draft' Pakistan-mediated text (REPORTED — Saudi state-influenced primary leak; Tasnim/ILNA confirmed draft moving but trace to same chain): immediate comprehensive unconditional ceasefire + Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz freedom-of-navigation guarantees + sanctions lifted in phases on Iranian compliance + talks within 7 days. **The text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium dismantlement** — Trump's May 16 letter central demand omitted. (3) **IRGC May 21 'global offensive' threat (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Iran International May 21, The Week May 20, CBS News liveblog)**: 'war beyond Middle East' framing escalates beyond prior IRIB May 19 'retreat-based-on-fear' framing. **No operational follow-through verified May 21-22** (no missile launches, no proxy attack scaling beyond established patterns). Tag holds at RHETORIC. (4) **Iranian state-media institutional divergence operationally active**: Tasnim (IRGC-linked) ran Iran's counter-proposal procedural framing (per The Globe Security, NewKerala, ProKerala May 19-22) emphasizing the deliverables Iran wants — end of war / sanctions lift / frozen-assets release / end of US blockade / war reparations. IRNA (civilian-government-aligned) covered Naqvi-Araghchi meetings in operational-procedural framing without amplifying IRGC's 'global offensive' rhetoric. **The internal divergence flagged May 19 brief is now operationally active**: civilian-government procedurally engaged with Pakistan track; IRGC and Khamenei's office rhetorically + substantively NO. (5) **IRGC Hormuz transit claim May 22**: 35 vessels passed in 24 hours (CLAIMED — IRGC primary, Iran International secondary). Independent maritime trackers (hormuzstraitmonitor.com, IranWarLive, Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker May 17) continue to characterize commercial shipping as 'largely frozen' — 35 vessels claim likely refers to ALL transits including Iranian coastal/cabotage, NOT international VLCC/tanker traffic. 1,550 stranded vessels and 22,500 trapped mariners headline figure remains operative. (6) **Mojtaba public-silence trigger advances to Day 15** with the public-silence pattern intact but the leak-architecture-voice pattern now operative. The May 21 directive (if real) functions as REGIME POLICY without requiring Mojtaba to appear publicly. (7) **Rial divergence trigger (Day 5 of 5) resolving UNTRIPPED**: Alanchand 1,775K May 19, Bonbast last May 15 1,822K — divergence narrowing not widening; deal-anticipation capping decline pace; 2M trigger ETA pushed past mid-June. **Mechanism inside the deal architecture**: Trump's May 16 letter required full dismantlement + zero enrichment within 2 months; the leaked Al Arabiya text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium; Mojtaba's directive (if real) makes the substantive nuclear track non-deliverable. The asymmetric path Iran appears to want: ceasefire deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum operational expression**: civilian (Pezeshkian/Araghchi) procedurally engaged via Pakistan; IRGC + Khamenei's office substantively NO. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated by Trump's 'no hurry' framing** — Iran did NOT have to publicly concede anything to keep the diplomatic track alive across the postponement window. The Mojtaba directive converts the nuclear-track non-deliverable from 'regime collapse risk' into a 'principled red line,' making it politically tenable to accept ceasefire-and-sanctions-lift while preserving the nuclear program. **Falsifiable**: Iran formally accepts the leaked Al Arabiya text within 7 days without nuclear amendments = thesis confirmed (Iran got the asymmetric split it wanted). Iran softens the Mojtaba directive within 14 days = thesis wrong (directive was bargaining noise, not a floor).

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP POSTPONED MAY 19 STRIKE AT GULF REQUEST — IRAN STATE TV (IRIB) CALLED IT A 'RETREAT' BASED ON 'FEAR.' FM BAGHAEI CONFIRMED COUNTER-PROPOSAL DELIVERED THROUGH PAKISTAN; QESHM ISLAND DEFENSE SYSTEMS REPORTEDLY ACTIVATED LATE MONDAY.** Iran's two-track information posture operationally crystallized today: state media hardens the rhetorical frame ('retreat-based-on-fear' via IRIB broadcast ticker and X account); institutional channels keep procedurally moving the counter-proposal through Pakistan. **FM spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei (via Tasnim primary)**: Iran's response to recent US proposals was 'conveyed to the American side through mediator Pakistan' with enumerated demands: (i) unfreezing of frozen Iranian assets, (ii) lifting of US sanctions on Iranian oil sales, (iii) lifting of US blockade of Iranian ports, (iv) 'stoppage of fighting in all aspects' including cessation in Lebanon, and (v) compensation for war damages — far apart from US zero-enrichment red line. **The Pakistan channel is procedural-real, framework-draft-pending.** Trump's postponement post (late May 18 US time) credited Qatar Emir Tamim, Saudi MbS, and UAE MbZ for the request — Iran NOT publicly credited as initiator. **IRIB 'retreat based on fear' framing**: converts the Gulf-mediated postponement into an Iranian rhetorical win, deepening the trust deficit on the negotiating track. This is the cleanest publicly-stated example of the Iranian 'negotiate while reframing the postponement as adversary weakness' pattern — Sadjadpour's 'two hubris' frame applies in real-time. **Defense systems reportedly activated on Iran's Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz late Monday** (REPORTED single-source from Trump-postponement coverage), with Iranian officials reporting situation 'under control' — Qeshm is Iran's largest Gulf island, hosts IRGC Navy facilities and surface-to-air missile batteries; activation is a CONFIRMED operational-readiness posture against potential US/Israeli strikes through the Hormuz corridor. **Consistent with Trump's 'remain prepared for full-scale assault at a moment's notice'** — Iran reading the postponement as conditional and behaving accordingly. **A9 MOJTABA KHAMENEI DAY 12 STATE-MEDIA SILENCE**: CNN piece 'Iran's new supreme leader is nowhere to be seen — that might be helping the regime to survive' re-circulates April 21 framing. Pezeshkian's May 7 claim of meeting Mojtaba 'for two and a half hours' remains uncorroborated by any image, audio, or third-party witness. Tasnim Abdollahi-briefing May 17 was second state-media positioning act in five days naming Mojtaba in operational-command function without producing him. **Deepens the structural problem with the Pakistan mediation track**: even if a framework draft is produced, who in the Iranian institutional architecture has the authority to sign it?
